prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "B-Skip 跑姿练习" (B-skip running drill) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person at the SIGNATURE LEG-EXTENSION + PAW-DOWN phase of a B-skip drill: right foot on the ball of the foot supporting on the ground. The LEFT LEG is the active drill leg and is in the DEFINING B-SKIP shape: left thigh is HELD AT PARALLEL to the floor (knee at hip height) AND the LEFT LOWER LEG (shin) IS FULLY EXTENDED FORWARD with the knee almost straightened — the foot is pointed forward and slightly down as if pawing or kicking forward, ready to whip back toward the ground. So the entire left leg is extended out in front, thigh horizontal and shin extending out from there, almost like a kicking motion. Arms in running-style opposition. Torso upright with slight forward lean. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the LEFT LEG IS NOT BENT 90° (that would be A-skip) — the lower leg is EXTENDED FORWARD AT THE KNEE in a forward-paw position. This is the visual difference from A-skip.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hamstring group of the extending leg (eccentric loading) and hip flexors and calf muscles. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腘绳肌" pointing to the hamstrings of the extending leg; "髂腰肌" pointing to the hip flexors; "臀大肌 / 小腿" pointing to the glute and calf area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
